county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Oregon holds records from 1836. Ogle County is on the Rock River — the town of Oregon was a center of the 19th-century arts community (Lorado Taft's Eagle's Nest colony). Black Hawk War (1832) military routes passed through. Check Jo Daviess County for pre-1836 records. Ogle County Historical Society. White Pines Forest State Park area. Strong New England and New York settlement. IRAD at Northern Illinois University. Polo and Rochelle are railroad towns with German and Scandinavian heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1836. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1836. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1836. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Ogle County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Peoria holds records from 1825. Peoria is the oldest European settlement in Illinois — French Fort Creve Coeur (1680) built by La Salle. The phrase 'Will it play in Peoria?' reflects the city's Midwestern character. Peoria was a major whiskey distilling center — Hiram Walker and other distillery records. Caterpillar Inc. (founded 1925) employment records. Peoria County Historical Society and Bradley University archives. IRAD at Illinois State University. African American community from Great Migration.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1825. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1825. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1825. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Peoria County Clerk, Peoria / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1825. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Peoria County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Military Tract Bounty Land Records — Peoria County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Military Tract between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers was reserved for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants. Many warrants were sold to speculators. Patent and warrant records identify original claimants and purchasers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-documented — patents at BLM-GLO; warrant records at NARA

source_profiles.repository: Peoria County Circuit Clerk, Peoria / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: African American Church and Community Records — Peoria County
source_profiles.repository: Vivian G. Harsh Research Collection (Chicago Public Library) / local churches and historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: African American church records (AME, Baptist, COGIC), fraternal organization records, and community newspapers. The Great Migration (1910-1970) brought hundreds of thousands of African Americans from the South to Illinois, particularly Chicago and industrial cities. Chicago Defender newspaper is a critical source.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Vivian G. Harsh Collection at Chicago Public Library is the largest African American history collection in the Midwest

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pinckneyville holds records from 1827. Perry County became a significant coal mining county — mine employment records at Illinois State Archives. Pinckneyville is the county seat. Created from Randolph and Jackson — check both for pre-1827 records. Du Quoin (shared with Jackson County) was a railroad center. Perry County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Du Quoin State Fair records document agricultural heritage. Early Upland South settlers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1827. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / Illinois Department of Mines and Minerals
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspection reports, employment records, accident reports, and company records. Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. Annual coal reports published by the State of Illinois list mines and operators.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine inspection records at Illinois State Archives; some company records in local collections

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Monticello holds records from 1841. Created from De Witt and Macon — check both for pre-1841 records. Piatt County is named for the Piatt brothers who were early settlers. Monticello is a classic central Illinois courthouse town. Allerton Park (Robert Allerton's estate, now University of Illinois property) is in Piatt County. Piatt County Historical and Genealogical Society. IRAD at the University of Illinois. Agricultural heritage. Friends Creek settlement. Strong Presbyterian and Methodist records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1841. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1841. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives

source_profiles.source_name: Eighth Judicial Circuit Court Records — Piatt County
source_profiles.repository: Piatt County Circuit Clerk, Monticello / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Abraham Lincoln practiced law on Illinois' Eighth Judicial Circuit from 1837 to 1860. Circuit court records from this period include cases Lincoln argued and contemporaries he worked with. Court records also document the legal, economic, and social history of central Illinois families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Circuit court records at county level; Lincoln Legal Papers project has compiled many Lincoln-related documents

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pittsfield holds records from 1821. Pike County was the original 'mother county' of western Illinois — many western Illinois counties were carved from Pike. Early settlers from Kentucky, Virginia, and New England. Pittsfield was an important county seat. Pike County Historical Society. IRAD at Western Illinois University. Military Tract land for War of 1812 veterans. Illinois River and Mississippi River communities. Check Madison, Bond, and Clark for pre-1821 records. Strong Baptist and Methodist heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Golconda holds records from 1816. Pope County is in the Shawnee National Forest — rugged terrain with Ohio River frontage. Golconda on the Ohio River was an important river town. Dixon Springs was a 19th-century health resort. Check Gallatin and Johnson for pre-1816 records. Pope County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Trail of Tears — Cherokee passed through Pope County. Upland South settlers from Kentucky and Tennessee. Fluorspar mining heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1816. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Mound City holds records from 1843. Mound City on the Ohio River was a Union Navy yard during the Civil War — gunboat construction and naval hospital records at NARA. Check Alexander and Johnson counties for pre-1843 records. Pulaski County had a significant African American community — Pulaski and Mound City records are important for Black family research in southern Illinois. Pulaski County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Ohio River commerce records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1843. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hennepin holds records from 1825. Putnam County is Illinois' smallest county by area. Hennepin is on the Illinois River — Hennepin Canal connects the Illinois and Rock Rivers. Putnam County was an early 'mother county' from which Bureau, Marshall, and Stark were carved. Check Fulton for pre-1825 records. Putnam County Historical Society. IRAD at Illinois State University. Illinois River commerce records. Small population means relatively complete record coverage. Strong agricultural heritage.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.source_name: Illinois & Michigan Canal Records — Putnam County
source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / I&M Canal National Heritage Corridor
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land sales, and worker rolls. The I&M Canal (1836-1848) connected Lake Michigan to the Illinois River. Canal construction employed thousands of Irish immigrant laborers. Canal land grant records document property sales along the corridor.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Illinois State Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1795 from St. Clair, Northwest Territory.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Chester holds records from the 1790s — Randolph is one of Illinois' two original counties (with St. Clair). French colonial heritage: Kaskaskia (first capital of Illinois, now nearly destroyed by Mississippi River flooding) and Prairie du Rocher have Catholic parish registers from the 1700s. Fort de Chartres records. Pierre Menard home. Chester is the birthplace of Popeye creator E.C. Segar. Randolph County Genealogical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. French, American, and German heritage layers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1795. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1795. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1795. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1795. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: French Colonial Catholic Parish Records — Randolph County
source_profiles.repository: Diocese of Belleville / Illinois State Archives / Newberry Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: French Catholic parish registers from the Illinois Country — baptisms, marriages, and burials. Cahokia (1699), Kaskaskia (1703), and Prairie du Rocher parishes have records predating American settlement. Records in French and Latin. These are among the oldest church records in the Midwest.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish registers well-preserved; some transcribed and published; microfilm copies at multiple repositories

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Randolph County

source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Olney holds records from 1841. Olney is famous for its white squirrels but has deeper genealogical roots. Created from Clay and Lawrence — check both for pre-1841 records. Richland County is in southeastern Illinois with Upland South settlement patterns. Oil production generated land records in the 20th century. Richland County Genealogical Society. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Carnegie Library in Olney has local history collections. Baptist and Methodist church records important.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1841. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Oil and Gas Lease Records — Richland County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease agreements recorded in county deed books. Illinois oil fields (particularly in southeastern Illinois) generated extensive lease and mineral rights records from the early 1900s onward. These records identify landowners and track property transactions.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved in county deed records
